title: Present Value of a Single Sum
event: Delgado Co. will receive $20,000 six years from now. The market rate is 10% (PV factor, 10%, 6 periods = 0.56447). Compute the present value.

computationPaths.narration: A dollar later is worth less than a dollar now — discounting strips out the interest that time would have earned.

memorize.body: PV = FV × PV factor = FV ÷ (1 + i)^n. FV = PV × (1 + i)^n — the factors are reciprocals.
Sanity check every answer's direction: PV < FV always (positive rates). If your 'present value' grew, you compounded instead of discounting.

distractors.feedback: You compounded FORWARD (× 1.77156). Money coming later gets discounted BACK: × 0.56447.
Simple-interest shortcut. TVM compounds — use the factor: 20,000 × 0.56447 = 11,289.
